articles.full_text:
Temperature nears 40°C as BMD forecasts multiple heatwaves in April
The temperature in Chuadanga has climbed to nearly 40 degrees Celsius at the start of April, the warmest month of the year, with the Bangladesh Meteorological Department (BMD) forecasting multiple heatwaves across the country in the coming weeks.
BMD said two to four mild to moderate heatwaves and one or two severe heatwaves may sweep across the country this month.
In areas experiencing mild heatwaves, temperatures range between 36 and 37.9 degrees Celsius. Temperatures between 38 and 39.9 degrees Celsius are considered moderate heatwaves, while 40 to 41.9 degrees Celsius are classified as severe.
According to this morning’s weather update, mild to moderate heatwaves are sweeping across 18 districts in Rajshahi and Khulna divisions, as well as Dhaka, Narayanganj, Manikganj, Faridpur, Gopalganj, Chandpur, Barishal, Patuakhali and Rangamati.
Yesterday, the highest temperature in the country was recorded at 39.7 degrees Celsius in Chuadanga, BMD said.
In 2024, a heatwave persisted for 36 consecutive days. Meteorologists believe conditions will not be as intense this year.
“There is a possibility of rain tomorrow and the day after, which may slightly reduce the temperature,” meteorologist Md Bazlur Rashid told The Daily Star.
“Heatwaves in April are normal. However, this year, there will not be a prolonged heatwave like in 2024,” he said.
The forecast also indicates that severe Nor’wester storms may occur sporadically this month. There is a likelihood of light to moderate rain and hailstorms on around seven days, and thunderstorms on one to three days.
“When it rains after a heat spell, cloud height is higher, which is why hailstorms occur. Once rainfall begins, this tendency decreases,” said meteorologist Md Tarifur Newaz Kabir.
He added that temperatures in the southwestern region are likely to be comparatively higher in April. However, due to the chances of rainfall, heatwaves are not expected to persist continuously.
In March, the average morning temperature was 0.90 degrees Celsius above normal. The lowest was recorded on March 4, when the minimum temperature in Rajarhat (Kurigram) dropped to 14.2 degrees Celsius, according to BMD.
However, the evening temperature was 0.4 degrees Celsius below normal. On March 27, the highest temperature was recorded at 37.8 degrees Celsius in Jashore.
Rainfall occurred on 24 days throughout March, with the highest rainfall of 83 millimetres recorded in Sylhet on March 13. Although overall rainfall was 31 percent above normal nationwide, it remained below normal in Khulna, Barishal and Chattogram divisions.
